article_title: SK Hynix Quintuples Q1 Operating Profit to a Record $25.4 Billion as AI Memory Demand Pushes the Industry Into a New Margin Regime

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: SK Hynix Quintuples Q1 Operating Profit to a Record $25.4 Billion as AI Memory Demand Pushes the Industry Into a New Margin Regime
content_preview.summary: South Korean memory giant reported 37.6 trillion won in operating profit on 52.6 trillion won in revenue, a 405% year-over-year jump and the highest operating margin in its history at 72%, as HBM and high-density server DRAM rode an AI-driven demand wave.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

SK Hynix on April 23 reported the largest quarterly profit in its history, with operating income rising more than five-fold from a year earlier as the world's leading high-bandwidth memory (HBM) supplier rode an AI-driven demand wave. The Korean memory maker — Nvidia's primary HBM partner — posted a 72% operating margin, a level the memory industry has never sustained at scale across a full quarter.

The results come amid a wider memory supercycle that has lifted SK Hynix into opera...

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-2 rewrite resolves all six round-1 findings by removing the entire HBM4E Roadmap section, dropping the HBM4E framing from summary/overview/What-We-Don't-Know, and softening LPDDR6 ramp wording to match the press release's future tense. Word count adjusted to 915 (still in News range), structure intact, financial reporting still strong.
editor_notes.source_verification: Round-1 verification confirmed all financial figures, growth-driver attributions, product-specific claims (SOCAMM2 / 1cnm / LPDDR6 / PQC21 / Solidigm), agentic-AI quote, CNBC framing (record profit, in line with estimates as memory prices climb, AI memory shortage backdrop), and Tom's Hardware bonus figures (250 trillion won 2026 OP forecast, 35,000 employees, $477K/$900K bonus projections, Samsung union dynamic). The four HBM4E roadmap claims that were misattributed to the press release (sample H2 2026 / mass production 2027 / 'discussing with customers' quote / 1c-process core die / Yongin May→Feb 2027 acceleration) have all been removed cleanly. Re-verifying the still-cited claims against the round-1 snapshots: all of them remain in the snapshot text and verify exactly as before.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All round-1 attribution errors resolved. Bot also clarified the 1cnm process attribution to LPDDR6/SOCAMM2 only (where the press release places it) instead of leaving it ambiguous. The 'plans to fully ramp up' wording matches the press release's future tense. No new findings.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Unchanged — neutral, analytical, hedged on supercycle duration, Samsung/Micron competitive trajectory, capacity-expansion timelines. The 'capacity-bound, not capital-bound' framing still closes the article and is well-supported by the data that remains.
editor_notes.originality: Same as round 1 — new topic, no overlap with prior MH coverage.
concerns: The HBM4E roadmap is real news that the bot confirmed independently via Korean wire services, GuruFocus transcript, and BigGo, but those sources are not on the source allowlist. The bot made the editorially correct call to remove rather than cite from a non-allowlisted source. Editorial team may want to consider adding Korea Herald, Korea Times, AJU Press, Seoul Economic Daily, or Trendforce to the allowlist for future SK Hynix earnings coverage.
recommendations: Optional process note: consider adding Korean financial wires (Korea Herald, Korea Times, AJU Press, Seoul Economic Daily) and Trendforce to config/source_allowlist.txt — they are the canonical English-language sources for SK Hynix earnings call disclosures and would have unblocked the HBM4E roadmap section.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean round-2 fix. The article is tighter and more rigorous: every remaining claim now maps to a citation that holds up. The bot's editorial judgment to remove rather than cite from non-allowlisted Korean wires shows good understanding of the allowlist constraint. APPROVE — ready to merge.
round_1_findings_resolved: HBM4E sample H2 2026 / mass production 2027 — section removed (could not be sourced from an allowlisted outlet).
Direct quote 'discussing the shipment schedule and product specifications for HBM4E with customers' — removed.
HBM4E core die uses 10nm-class 6th-gen (1c) process — removed; 1cnm process now correctly tied only to LPDDR6/SOCAMM2.
Yongin cleanroom advancement May→Feb 2027 — removed; bullet rewritten to reflect only what the press release actually says ('infrastructure preparation on the Yongin cluster', no milestone).
'Started full ramp-up of LPDDR6 shipments' — softened to 'plans to fully ramp up shipments of LPDDR6' to match press release future tense.

Headline/summary/Overview HBM4E framing — title unchanged (was financial-only); summary rewritten to keep financial framing and add the sourced 72% margin; Overview second sentence rewritten to drop HBM4E and add the 72% margin.
